executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented coordinated ransomware campaign affecting over 100 organizations in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and Kids & Company childcare. The Qilin ransomware group leads with 21 victims, followed by Play (11), Safepay (11), and Clop (13). Critical sectors hit include healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and manufacturing—suggesting either a mass exploitation event or coordinated supply-chain compromise targeting Canadian infrastructure.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a massive coordinated ransomware wave affecting over 100 organizations across all sectors and provinces in a 48-hour window. High-impact victims include Canada Goose (consumer brand), National Money Mart (financial services, database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, MRC de Maskinongé district government, and Municipality of North Perth. No CCCS advisories were identified in this feed period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (Coinbasecartel), Active Green + Ross (Sinobi), Kids & Company childcare services (Sinobi)
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company - database leaked (Everest), Morison Insurance Brokers (Sinobi), Trucash financial services (Lynx), multiple accounting firms including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, ReidhurstNagy

report_2w.executive_summary: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager has a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-20245) that is being actively exploited in the wild to gain root access. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued an advisory about FortiBleed, a massive leak of thousands of compromised Fortinet device credentials. ShinyHunters breached Madison Square Garden, exposing 9.8 million records including facial recognition data. Multiple ransomware groups (Akira, Qilin) continue to target organizations globally. LastPass suffered yet another data breach, further eroding trust in the password manager.

items.description: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager vulnerability allowing unauthorized root access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Cisco CUCM WebDialer SSRF allowing unauthenticated remote root file-writes via Tomcat
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Path traversal vulnerability in Apache HTTP Server
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: Medium, Availability: Low

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Madison Square Garden Sports (9.8M records including facial recognition data), NAIC.org
items.ttps: T1566 Phishing (vishing attack via phone call), T1589 Gather Victim Identity Information, T1213 Data from Information Repositories

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: JMS Southeast, Padget Technologies
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: ISOPLUS
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery, T1567.002 Exfiltration to Cloud Storage

items.name: KongTuke (WoodGnat)
items.targets: Multiple sectors via ClickFix and ModeloRAT campaigns
items.ttps: T1204 User Execution (ClickFix social engineering), T1547 Boot or Logon Autostart Execution, T1071 Application Layer Protocol (Mistic backdoor)

items.name: MuddyWater (Iran-linked APT)
items.targets: Various organizations globally, masquerading as ransomware gang
items.ttps: T1485 Data Destruction (fake ransomware), T1027 Obfuscated Files or Information, T1071 Application Layer Protocol

items.name: PEAR Ransomware
items.targets: Optimum First Mortgage (9.3 TB data theft)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: Prinz Eugen Ransomware
items.targets: Various organizations
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact (Go-based encryptor), T1490 Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: Nation-state actor (unattributed)
items.targets: Australia's Rural Fire Service, SD-WAN infrastructure globally (CVE-2026-20245 exploitation)
items.ttps: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1068 Exploitation for Privilege Escalation, T1078 Valid Accounts

items.name: Bluekit Phishing Kit Operators
items.targets: Multiple organizations via browser-in-the-middle attacks
items.ttps: T1566.002 Phishing: Spearphishing Link, T1557 Adversary-in-the-Middle, T1539 Steal Web Session Cookie

items.name: AWS AiTM Phishing Kit Operators
items.targets: AWS console users between June 16-19, 2026
items.ttps: T1566 Phishing, T1557 Adversary-in-the-Middle, T1539 Steal Web Session Cookie, T1556 Modify Authentication Process (MFA bypass)

items.name: ClickFix Campaign Operators
items.targets: macOS users via fake CAPTCHA pages
items.ttps: T1204.002 User Execution: Malicious File (DMG execution), T1566.002 Phishing: Spearphishing Link, T1056 Input Capture (infostealer)

items.name: SocGholish Malware Operators
items.targets: Multiple organizations (Operation Endgame disrupted 106 servers/domains, ~15,000 compromised systems, 27M credentials)
items.ttps: T1189 Drive-by Compromise, T1204 User Execution, T1071 Application Layer Protocol

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-014 regarding FortiBleed, a significant leak of thousands of compromised Fortinet device credentials that impacts Canadian organizations using Fortinet infrastructure. No specific breaches of major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period. Canadian organizations should prioritize patching Fortinet devices and reviewing access credentials immediately.
